Examples.Title: Add memories (V3)
Examples.Description: Extract and store memories from a conversation using the V3 additive pipeline. Entity IDs (`user_id` / `agent_id` / `run_id`) are accepted at the top level. At least one entity ID is required so the memory is scoped to a session.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Mem0;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Add memories.
Examples.Code:
### Add memories.
# @name memories_create
POST {{host}}/v1/memories/
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "messages": [
    {}
  ],
  "agent_id": "string",
  "user_id": "string",
  "app_id": "string",
  "run_id": "string",
  "metadata": {},
  "includes": "string",
  "excludes": "string",
  "infer": true,
  "output_format": "v1.1",
  "custom_categories": {},
  "custom_instructions": "string",
  "immutable": false,
  "async_mode": true,
  "timestamp": 0,
  "expiration_date": "2025-01-01",
  "org_id": "string",
  "project_id": "string",
  "version": "string"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Successful memory creation.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad Request. Invalid input data. Please refer to the memory creation documentation at https://docs.mem0.ai/platform/quickstart#4-1-create-memories for correct formatting and required fields.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Get all memories (V3, paginated)
Examples.Description: List memories scoped by filters, paginated. Entity IDs **must** be passed inside the `filters` object — top-level `user_id` / `agent_id` / `run_id` are rejected with 400. `filters` supports the same operator set as V2 search (`AND`, `OR`, `NOT`, `in`, `gte`, `lte`, etc.). Response is a paginated envelope; pass `page` and `page_size` as query parameters to step through results.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Mem0;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Search memories (V3)
Examples.Description: Relevance-ranked search across stored memories. V3 uses hybrid retrieval — the returned `score` is a combined `[0, 1]` value; per-signal component scores are not exposed on the response. Entity IDs **must** be passed inside the `filters` object — top-level `user_id` / `agent_id` / `run_id` are rejected with 400. At least one entity ID is required.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Mem0;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.
